summary: Maintainer seeds three issues of different types (a bug accept, a feature needing design, a docs accept). With the skill loaded the orchestrator fans out one read-only triage child per issue, applies the confidence gate, and renders ONE consolidated digest covering all three (not three drop-by-drop reviews); only after the single maintainer reply does it implement the approved rows via the B2 type router. Without the skill, the baseline reviews issues one at a time and uses a single generic implementation path regardless of type.

scenario.user_query: autopilot these to merge with one triage review for the batch: #520 (bug: wrong exit code on lint failure), #521 (feature: pluggable cache backend), #522 (docs: document the new --quiet flag)
expected_behavior_with_skill: one triage child per issue, read-only, returning autopilot-triage-decision JSON
521 (needs-design) routes to gate=escalate; 520 and 522 route to auto-proceed
exactly ONE consolidated digest covers all three rows with a single maintainer checkpoint
implement router loads implement-bug.md for 520 and implement-docs.md for 522, not a generic path
521 is left for the maintainer, never auto-implemented

rubric.description: Exactly one consolidated review, not per-issue.
needs-design row is escalated, not implemented.
B2 router loads per-type lenses rather than a generic path.
Ground-truth table with the autopilot column header present.
